verificationNote: Risk register items are constructed examples for cloud computing environments based on real-world incidents (Capital One 2019, SolarWinds 2020, Codecov 2021, Uber 2022) and CSA CCM v4 controls. Organisations must develop their own risk register based on site-specific assessment. Not endorsed by any standards body.
title: Cloud Security Risk Register
description: 15 cloud-specific risks covering identity, data, network, compliance, operational, and supply chain categories. Each risk includes inherent and residual scoring, existing controls, CCM control mappings, and treatment plans.

risks.title: IAM misconfiguration enabling privilege escalation
risks.description: Overly permissive IAM policies, wildcard permissions, or misconfigured role trust relationships allow an attacker to escalate privileges from a low-privilege account to administrative access. IAM misconfiguration is the root cause of the majority of cloud security incidents (Capital One 2019 -- SSRF exploited overly permissive IAM role).
existingControls: CSP default guardrails (AWS SCPs, Azure Policy)
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement least-privilege IAM policies using automated policy analysis tools (AWS IAM Access Analyzer, Azure AD PIM). Enforce permission boundaries (SCPs/permission boundaries). Deploy CSPM with continuous IAM posture assessment. Conduct quarterly IAM policy audits. Eliminate wildcard permissions in all production accounts.

risks.title: Data exposure through misconfigured cloud storage
risks.description: Cloud storage services (AWS S3, Azure Blob Storage, GCP Cloud Storage) configured with public read access expose sensitive data to the internet. Automated scanners continuously probe for misconfigured storage. Incidents include exposed customer databases, credentials, and regulated data.
existingControls: CSP block public access settings at account level
Storage access policies reviewed during provisioning
risks.treatmentPlan: Enable account-level block public access on all storage services. Deploy CSPM with real-time alerting on public storage detection. Implement IaC templates that enforce private-only storage by default. Conduct weekly automated scans for public storage. Classify all data in cloud storage and enforce access policies by classification.
risks.owner: Cloud Security Engineer / Data Protection Officer

risks.title: Cryptojacking through compromised cloud compute
risks.description: Attacker compromises cloud compute instances or serverless functions to mine cryptocurrency. Compromised instances consume compute resources, generate unexpected costs, and indicate broader security failures (credential compromise, vulnerability exploitation). Costs can escalate rapidly -- USD 100,000+ bills have been reported.
existingControls: Compute instance security groups restrict inbound access
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement real-time billing anomaly detection with automated alerts (AWS Budgets, Azure Cost Alerts). Deploy CSP-native threat detection (GuardDuty, Defender for Cloud, Security Command Center) to detect cryptomining behaviour. Restrict compute instance creation through IAM policies. Implement network egress controls to block known mining pool destinations.

risks.title: Supply chain compromise through CI/CD pipeline
risks.description: Attacker compromises the CI/CD pipeline to inject malicious code into cloud deployments. Compromised build environments, poisoned container images, or tampered IaC templates can deploy backdoors across the entire cloud estate. SolarWinds (2020) and Codecov (2021) demonstrated the scale of CI/CD supply chain attacks.
existingControls: Code review process for pull requests
Container image scanning in CI pipeline
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement code signing and verification for all deployment artefacts. Use immutable container images from a trusted private registry with automated vulnerability scanning. Deploy SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generation and monitoring. Enforce branch protection rules and mandatory code reviews. Audit CI/CD service account permissions quarterly. Implement pipeline integrity monitoring.

risks.title: Data sovereignty violation through cross-region replication
risks.description: Cloud service replicates data to regions outside permitted jurisdictions, violating data residency requirements. Some CSP services replicate metadata globally by default. Malaysian PDPA, BNM RMiT, and NACSA Act 854 impose data residency requirements for regulated data.
existingControls: Cloud region configuration set to approved regions
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement region restriction policies (AWS SCPs, Azure Policy, GCP Organisation Policies) to prevent resource creation in non-approved regions. Audit all cloud services for default cross-region replication behaviour. Deploy data residency monitoring with automated alerting on policy violations. Maintain data residency compliance register per service. Review CSP data processing agreements for data location guarantees.
risks.owner: Cloud Governance Lead / DPO / Compliance Manager

risks.title: Shadow IT and unmanaged cloud sprawl
risks.description: Business units provision cloud services outside IT governance, creating unmanaged cloud accounts with no security controls, no monitoring, and no compliance oversight. Shadow cloud usage bypasses security architecture, IAM policies, and data protection controls.
existingControls: Periodic IT spend review for cloud charges
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) to discover and monitor all cloud service usage. Establish centralised cloud account provisioning with mandatory security baseline. Deploy network-level cloud service discovery via proxy logs and DNS analysis. Conduct quarterly cloud account inventory reconciliation. Implement financial controls (procurement approval for cloud spending).
risks.owner: Cloud Governance Lead / IT Security Manager

risks.title: Insider threat through privileged cloud access
risks.description: Malicious or negligent insider with privileged cloud access exfiltrates data, modifies infrastructure, or disables security controls. Cloud environments concentrate significant power in administrative accounts -- a single admin can access all resources across the estate.
existingControls: Administrative actions logged to CloudTrail/Activity Log
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement just-in-time privileged access (AWS IAM Identity Center, Azure PIM) with approval workflows. Deploy UEBA (User and Entity Behaviour Analytics) on cloud audit logs to detect anomalous administrative patterns. Enforce separation of duties for critical operations (account deletion, data export). Implement break-glass procedures with post-use review for emergency access. Quarterly access certification for all privileged accounts.

risks.title: API key exposure through source code or logs
risks.description: Cloud API keys, service account credentials, or secrets are accidentally committed to source code repositories, embedded in container images, or written to log files. Exposed credentials are harvested within minutes by automated scanners on public repositories.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement centralised secrets management (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager, HashiCorp Vault). Deploy pre-commit hooks for secret detection (git-secrets, gitleaks). Enable CSP credential exposure detection (AWS compromised key detection). Automate credential rotation with short-lived tokens. Prohibit long-lived API keys in favour of IAM roles with temporary credentials. Monitor public repositories for organisation credential exposure.

risks.title: Container escape from multi-tenant environment
risks.description: Attacker exploits container runtime vulnerability to escape container isolation and access the host OS or neighbouring containers. In shared Kubernetes clusters or container services, this enables cross-tenant access. Container escapes have been demonstrated in Docker (CVE-2019-5736), runc, and containerd.
existingControls: Container images from trusted registries only
Container runtime updated to latest stable version
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce non-root container execution. Implement Kubernetes Pod Security Standards (restricted profile). Deploy runtime security monitoring (Falco, Sysdig). Use gVisor or Kata Containers for workloads requiring stronger isolation. Restrict container capabilities to minimum required set. Automate container runtime patching. For sensitive workloads, use dedicated node pools with no co-tenancy.

risks.title: Serverless function injection
risks.description: Attacker exploits input validation vulnerabilities in serverless functions (AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, GCP Cloud Functions) to inject malicious payloads. Serverless injection can lead to data exfiltration, privilege escalation, or denial of service. Event-driven invocation with untrusted inputs increases injection risk.
existingControls: Serverless function execution role with limited permissions
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce strict input validation and output encoding for all serverless functions. Apply least-privilege execution roles -- each function gets only the permissions it needs. Deploy serverless-aware application security testing (DAST/SAST) in CI/CD pipeline. Implement function timeout and concurrency limits to prevent denial-of-service. Monitor function invocation patterns for anomalies.

risks.title: MFA bypass on cloud administrative accounts
risks.description: Attacker bypasses multi-factor authentication on cloud administrative accounts through SIM swapping, MFA fatigue attacks (push notification bombing), session token theft, or phishing with real-time MFA relay (AiTM phishing). The Uber 2022 breach used MFA fatigue to compromise an admin account.
existingControls: MFA enabled on all administrative accounts
risks.treatmentPlan: Upgrade from push-notification MFA to phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn hardware security keys) for all administrative accounts. Implement number-matching MFA to prevent fatigue attacks. Deploy conditional access policies with device compliance, impossible travel detection, and IP-based restrictions. Monitor for anomalous authentication patterns. Implement session token binding to prevent token theft.

risks.title: Cross-tenant data leakage in shared cloud services
risks.description: Vulnerability in CSP shared infrastructure or multi-tenant service enables data leakage between tenants. Cross-tenant attacks have been demonstrated in shared database services, caching layers, and serverless execution environments. The organisation has limited visibility into CSP-side isolation controls.
existingControls: CSP SOC 2 Type II report reviewed annually
Data encrypted at rest with customer-managed keys
risks.treatmentPlan: For high-sensitivity workloads, use dedicated/isolated instance types (dedicated hosts, isolated VPCs). Encrypt all data with customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) -- even if cross-tenant access occurs, data remains encrypted. Monitor CSP security advisories for multi-tenancy vulnerabilities. Review CSP SOC 2 Type II reports for tenant isolation control effectiveness. Evaluate CSP commitment to responsible disclosure.
risks.owner: Cloud Security Engineer / Cloud Governance Lead

risks.title: DNS hijacking of cloud-hosted services
risks.description: Attacker compromises DNS records to redirect traffic intended for cloud-hosted services to attacker-controlled infrastructure. Compromised DNS enables credential harvesting, data interception, and phishing. Cloud DNS configurations are managed via API and are vulnerable to credential compromise.
existingControls: DNS managed through cloud provider (Route 53, Azure DNS, Cloud DNS)
DNS changes logged in audit trail
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement DNSSEC for all externally-facing domains. Enable domain locking and transfer protection at registrar. Restrict DNS record modification to dedicated IAM roles with MFA. Monitor DNS record changes with automated alerting. Implement Certificate Transparency log monitoring to detect unauthorized certificate issuance for organisation domains.

risks.title: Cloud account takeover through credential theft
risks.description: Attacker gains full control of a cloud account (AWS account, Azure subscription, GCP project) through stolen root/owner credentials. Account takeover enables complete data exfiltration, resource destruction, and creation of persistent backdoor accounts. Recovery requires CSP intervention.
existingControls: Separate accounts for production and development
risks.treatmentPlan: Lock root/owner account with hardware MFA token stored in physical safe. Eliminate all root account API keys. Implement multi-account strategy with AWS Organisations/Azure Management Groups/GCP Organisation to limit blast radius. Deploy real-time alerting on root account usage. Establish CSP support relationship with priority incident response for account recovery. Maintain offline backup of critical account configuration.

risks.title: Insufficient logging and monitoring enabling undetected breach
risks.description: Cloud audit logging is disabled, improperly configured, or not monitored, enabling attackers to operate undetected for extended periods. The average cloud breach dwell time exceeds 200 days when logging is inadequate. Without CloudTrail/Activity Log/Audit Log, no forensic evidence exists for incident response.
existingControls: CloudTrail/Activity Log enabled on primary accounts
Basic alerting rules for critical events
risks.treatmentPlan: Enable comprehensive cloud audit logging across all accounts, all regions, and all services (including data-plane events for sensitive services). Centralise logs in immutable storage with tamper protection. Implement SIEM with cloud-specific detection rules covering CSA Top Threats. Deploy automated alerting for critical events (root login, IAM policy change, security group modification, storage policy change). Conduct quarterly log coverage audits. Ensure log retention meets NACSA s26 7-year requirement for NCII-designated operators.
